## Metro uses watchman that leaks watched files · Issue #1207 · facebook/metro
https://github.com/facebook/metro/issues/1207

In an new expo project created by "npx create-expo-app --template", when doing "expo start" the metro bundler uses watchman that watches over 100k files. Watching this amount of files takes 1Gb of RAM and Watchman keeps running after stopping expo.
...
> Hi, that's as designed - 100k files sounds about right for watching everything in a new project, that doesn't sound like a leak to me.
> 
> Watchman is deliberately persistent, so that tools that use it don't have to re-crawl all files every time they start. When Metro starts, if Watchman is already running, Metro can just quickly query it for changed files. Jest works in the same way, and can "share" Watchman with Metro.
> 
> If you prefer not to use Watchman you can turn it off with `resolver.useWatchman: false` in your `metro.config.js`, but note that this will mean increased startup times, especially if you frequently stop and start Expo CLI.
